text: We need to find and then add a keyboard shortcut (and command) for the #gui to collapse parent - in doing so, the caret should jump to the parent of the current task whose children are now collapsed
Bullets don't show priorities which breaks the vertical alignment of text - we need to plan for this and have a same sized gap (or, just show priorities for bullet points as well, why not?)
Kalamu #hub — one always-on local server, all projects in a sidebar (SPEC.md 'Hub' section is canonical)
Hub sidebar is render-once: project names/open-task badges only refresh on navigation — consider a hub-global SSE stream for live cross-project badge updates #hub #gui
A launchd-managed hub keeps running old server code after a CLI update (assets refresh, process doesn't) — consider the hub noticing a newer bundle on disk and exiting so KeepAlive restarts it #hub
Landing site (Astro, packages/landing)
Design + write real landing content (hero, live outline demo island, install, agent story) #landing
Deploy landing to kalamu.dev (CF Pages or Vercel, root dir packages/landing) + DNS at registrar #landing

Split/merge round-trip loses the boundary space when the caret sits after it ("Parent |Split"): commit-time whitespace normalization trims the before-half's trailing space, so a later Backspace-merge yields "ParentSplit". Exact round-trip would need the split commit to skip trailing-space normalization - core commit/tokens change, needs a decision.
Tag-chip colour popover inherits pre-wrap whitespace from the outline's .text, so its sections get ~22px line-box gaps (pre-existing; sidebar popover unaffected). Fix: white-space normal on .popover
Mod+Shift+3/4/5 are macOS screenshot shortcuts — the hub never sees them, so projects 3–5 aren't reachable by keyboard on a default Mac. Keep as-is, remap, or add a second binding?
Should kalamu init write the recommended .gitignore entries itself instead of only printing them?
Decision 2026-07-18: yes — init writes the .kalamu ignore entries itself, but only when a repo marker exists (looksLikeRepo); otherwise it keeps printing the suggestion

Shipped: cli/src/gitignore.ts (per-line idempotent, skips when .kalamu/ wholly ignored), --no-gitignore flag mirroring --no-agent-docs, runs on re-init too; SPEC files+init sections updated; 5 new tests

Show a `Copy [Mod] [C]` button on each node when it is focused. On hover, show the same button - but without the kbd part since the keyboard shortcut will only copy the node where the cursor is at that point in time
Should kalamu hub be folded into kalamu open (open starts/opens the hub by default)?

Decision 2026-07-18: keep both commands — open is project-scoped, hub is the machine-global server + lifecycle (install/uninstall/restart); merging would tie the global server to one terminal or push the login item, which SPEC forbids

Verified live: with a hub listening, kalamu open already routes to http://127.0.0.1:4400/p/<slug> ('hub is already serving this project'); the observed 4242 fallback happened because the foreground hub had been Ctrl+C'd first
Possible future tweak (not scheduled): when the hub is installed via launchd but not running, open could kick it awake instead of falling back to standalone
Recognise URLs inside text and when recognised underline them and make them clickable links.

Link segments in segments.ts: http(s):// only (no bare domains), trailing punctuation trimmed with paren-balancing, parsed before tags so URL #fragments never chip; display-mode anchors (new tab, noopener) reuse the data-chip path so clicks open the link without entering edit mode
Add a keyboard shortcut (and palette command) for the #gui to collapse parent - caret jumps to the parent of the current node whose children are now collapsed

Shortcut: Mod+Shift+Up (next to Mod+Up move-up; overrides macOS select-to-start inside the outline, same family as Mod+Up/Down). Palette: 'Collapse parent' as item 5, sheets stay last (SPEC palette section updated to ten items)

Inert on root-level nodes and the zoom root (nothing rendered above to fold); direct children of the zoom root fold the zoom root itself; palette action closes with focus on the parent, not the acted-on node
Inverse of collapse-parent for the #gui: Mod+Shift+Down expands the focused node's collapsed children and moves the caret into the first child; palette command 'Expand children'

Mod+Shift+Down mirrors collapse-parent exactly; already-expanded nodes get pure descent (repeated presses walk down the tree); filter/hideDone-aware first-child focus (never focuses a hidden node); palette item 6 'Expand children', view sheets now unbadged at 10/11
